tipRules.tip: Johnston: Use a drop shot with a tungsten weight and a 6th Sense Party Minnow on a BKK drop-shot hook. This is the primary technique for St. Lawrence smallmouth across shallow and deep zones.
Johnston: Mix in a Ned rig alongside the drop shot when targeting St. Lawrence River smallmouth as a secondary presentation.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, catch them shallow on sand flats, rockpiles, weed edges all the way to 35-40 feet on little high spots, rocks and deeper weed edges. There's not one specific thing — hit as many spots as you can until you run into them.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, my bigger fish (5.5 and 6.75 lbs) came shallow. Hit 12 spots and the last one had them — sometimes the late-day push to new water pays off.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, 24 pounds really isn't a good day — anything over 25 is what you need to win. It's a special place.
